result.summary: Redocly exposes a public MCP server at `https://redocly.com/mcp`, a documented CLI (`redocly` via npm/Docker) with CLI login and API-key-based push operations, and a machine-readable OpenAPI description for its generic Docs MCP interface.
result.description: Redocly provides tools for API documentation, developer portals, and API lifecycle workflows such as linting, bundling, publishing, and API cataloging. Its products include hosted documentation and portal tooling plus open source CLI utilities for working with OpenAPI and related specifications.

redocly_org_api_key.label: Redocly organization API key
redocly_org_api_key.setup: In your Redocly/Reunite project, create an organization API key from the API keys management page described in [Manage API keys](https://redocly.com/docs/realm/setup/how-to/api-keys). The CLI docs say to place the key in the `REDOCLY_AUTHORIZATION` environment variable before running `redocly push`.

redocly_cli_login.label: Redocly CLI login session
redocly_cli_login.setup: Run `redocly login` and complete the browser/device sign-in flow shown by the CLI. The docs show it opens or directs you to [device login](https://auth.cloud.redocly.com/device-login) and stores credentials for premium features. Note: the docs explicitly say this login does **not** let you use `redocly push` without a separate API key.

variables.name: projectHost
variables.description: Project host serving the Docs MCP server; if deployed under a path prefix, prepend that prefix to `/mcp`.
